title: Post-graduation work permit: Field of study requirement - Canada.ca
content:
Post-graduation work permit

## Field of study requirement
Depending on your study
    program , you may need to meet a field of study requirement to be eligible for a post-graduation work permit
  (PGWP).
You don’t need to meet the field of study requirement if one of the following
    applies to you:
- You submitted your PGWP application before November 1, 2024, regardless of level of
      study.
- You graduated from a PGWP-eligible flight school.
- You graduated with a bachelor’s degree, master’s degree or doctoral degree.
- You applied for a study permit before November 1, 2024.
If you don’t meet one of the exceptions above, use the table below to check if your program is eligible for a PGWP.
Special instructions to submit proof that you graduated in an eligible field of study
Make sure you know how to submit proof that you graduated in an eligible field of study in the online application .
- How to meet the field of study requirement
- Check if your CIP code is eligible
- Check the list of currently eligible CIP codes
- Search for your CIP code

## How to meet the field of study requirement
You must graduate from a program that’s on the list of currently eligible Classification of Instructional Programs (CIP) codes when you submit your
- study permit application or
Eligible fields of study are linked to certain jobs in long-term shortage in Canada and can change based on the needs of the Canadian labour market. If your CIP code is not on the list of currently eligible codes when you apply for a PGWP, it may still be eligible based on when you submitted your study permit application.
A CIP code is a number we use to classify post-secondary education programs according to the field of study.
For PGWP eligibility, we use 6-digit CIP codes based on the CIP Canada 2021 (opens in a new tab) .
To find your program’s CIP code, you can
- check your designated learning institution’s (DLI) website Most DLIs have information about PGWP eligibility and CIP codes on their website.
- Most DLIs have information about PGWP eligibility and CIP codes on their website.
- contact your DLI Contact your DLI for more information about the program and applicable 6-digit CIP codes if you have any questions.
- Contact your DLI for more information about the program and applicable 6-digit CIP codes if you have any questions.
- search the Statistics Canada CIP classification catalogue and find the 6-digit code that’s most applicable Search by keyword or by navigating through the available links in the main table. Program titles can be broad. Read the description, illustrative examples and exceptions found on the individual results to make sure your program matches.
- Search by keyword or by navigating through the available links in the main table.
- Program titles can be broad. Read the description, illustrative examples and exceptions found on the individual results to make sure your program matches.

## Check if your CIP code is eligible
To meet the field of study requirement, your CIP code must be on the list of currently eligible CIP codes when you submit either your study permit application or your PGWP application.

### PGWP field of study list freeze for 2026
For 2026, we won’t add or remove any eligible fields of study.
Check below to see if your program’s CIP code meets the field of study requirement.
To find out if your CIP code is eligible
- check the list of currently eligible CIP codes
- search for the CIP code for your specific study program

## Check the list of currently eligible CIP codes
Check if your program’s 6-digit CIP code is on the list of CIP codes that are eligible right now.

## Search for your CIP code
If you know the 6-digit CIP code for your program, you can search for it below.
